children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Director is authorized to expend funds appropriated for activities of the Institute in any fiscal year, to support, promote, and coordinate activities and efforts to enhance public awareness and understanding of measurement sciences, standards and technology at the national measurement laboratories and otherwise in fulfillment of the mission of the Institute. The Director shall carry out activities under this subsection, including education and outreach activities to the general public, industry and diverse types of institutions of higher education, including historically Black colleges and universities, Tribal Colleges and Universities, and minority-serving institutions, and community colleges in support of the Institute’s mission.
to ensure corporate memory of and expertise in the fundamental ongoing work, and on developing new capabilities in priority areas;
to maintain high overall technical competence;
to improve staff diversity;
to balance emphases on the noncore and core areas; or
to improve the ability of the Institute to compete in the marketplace for qualified personnel.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director may establish a program to use volunteers in carrying out the programs of the Institute.

children.label: A
children.text: is to be without compensation; and

children.label: B
children.text: will not be used to displace any current employee or act as a substitute for any future full-time employee of the Institute.

children.heading: Federal employee status
children.label: 3 Federal employee status
children.text: Any individual who provides voluntary service under this subsection shall not be considered a Federal employee, except for purposes of chapter 81 of title 5 (relating to compensation for injury), and sections 2671 through 2680 of title 28 (relating to tort claims).

children.label: A
children.text: students at institutions of higher learning within the United States who show promise as present or future contributors to the mission of the Institute; and

children.label: B
children.text: United States citizens for research and technical activities of the Institute, including programs.

children.heading: Selection criteria
children.label: 2 Selection criteria
children.text: The selection of persons to receive such fellowships and assistance shall be made on the basis of ability and of the relevance of the proposed work to the mission and programs of the Institute.

children.heading: Financial and logistical assistance
children.label: 3 Financial and logistical assistance
children.text: Notwithstanding or any other law to the contrary, the Director may include as a form of financial or logistical assistance under this subsection temporary housing and transportation to and from Institute facilities.section 1345 of title 31
facilitate education programs for undergraduate and graduate students, postdoctoral researchers, and academic and industry employees;
sponsor summer workshops for STEM kindergarten through grade 12 teachers as appropriate;
develop programs for graduate student internships and visiting faculty researchers;
document publications, presentations, and interactions with visiting researchers and sponsoring interns as performance metrics for improving and continuing interactions with those individuals;
facilitate laboratory tours and provide presentations for educational, industry, and community groups; and1So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.1

conduct outreach to and develop research collaborations with historically Black colleges and universities, Tribal Colleges or Universities, and minority serving institutions, including through the recruitment of students and faculty at such institutions to participate in programs developed under paragraph (3);

conduct outreach to and develop research collaborations with community colleges, including through the recruitment of students and faculty at such institutions to participate in programs developed under paragraph (3);
carry out other activities to increase the participation of persons historically underrepresented in STEM in the Institute’s programs; and

conduct outreach to and develop collaborations with nontraditional educational organizations, including those that offer training through nonprofit associations and professional associations or professional societies, to engage persons historically underrepresented in STEM through programs developed under this subsection.
